concept.conceptName: Alanine aminotransferase|Catalytic Concentration|Moment in time|Blood, Serum or Plasma

en.name: Alanine aminotransferase (ALT) in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Alanine aminotransferase (ALT, also called SGPT — serum glutamic pyruvic transaminase) is an enzyme produced primarily in the liver, with smaller amounts in the heart, skeletal muscle, kidney and pancreas [1]. When hepatocytes are damaged, ALT leaks into the bloodstream and its serum activity rises — making it the most specific routine marker of hepatocellular injury (viral hepatitis, drug-induced liver injury, ischaemic hepatitis, etc.) [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a biochemistry analyser on serum or plasma. It is collected in routine biochemistry as part of every liver panel. Results are reported as catalytic activity concentration; NPU recognises both **U/L** (the conventional unit, used by virtually all clinical labs) and **µkat/L** (the SI unit) [2]. The IFCC reference measurement procedure for ALT at 37 °C, listed by JCTLM, gives the conversion factor `1 U/L = 0.01667 µkat/L` [3]. **Note on assay method (with vs without P-5'-P).** Pyridoxal-5'-phosphate (P-5'-P) is the cofactor of ALT. The IFCC primary reference procedure adds P-5'-P to the reagent to activate the enzyme that is otherwise present as inactive apo-ALT, giving the total ALT activity [4]. Assays run without added P-5'-P measure only the holo-fraction already saturated in the sample, which can underestimate total activity (especially in patients deficient in vitamin B6). Both methods produce results expressed in U/L; values are not strictly interchangeable across the two methods, but LOINC treats them as the same measurand under different method specifications. ## Included Concepts The set contains five LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, all measuring ALT catalytic activity in blood, differing by specimen and by whether P-5'-P is added. ### Generic (method not specified) The default when the source does not state which method is used. - *3006923 / 1742-6 — Alanine aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: most widely used LOINC code, returned by routine biochemistry analysers when the method is not detailed. ### Method-specific (with or without P-5'-P) Use these only when the source explicitly identifies the assay method. - *3005755 / 1743-4 — Alanine aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma by With P-5'-P (LOINC)*: IFCC-recommended method. - *3027388 / 1744-2 — Alanine aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma by No addition of P-5'-P (LOINC)*: economy method, still common in practice. ### Other specimen specifications - *46235106 / 76625-3 — Alanine aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Blood (LOINC)*: used when the source reports ALT on whole blood without specifying serum/plasma. - *46236949 / 77144-4 — Alanine aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum, Plasma or Blood (LOINC)*: generic concept used when the matrix is not differentiated between serum/plasma and whole blood (e.g. aggregated multi-source feeds). **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC: **`[U]/L`** (concept 8645). The notations `U/L` and `IU/L` are used interchangeably in clinical practice. ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded. The set is defined by anchoring on the LOINC group for ALT catalytic activity in blood/serum/plasma and pulling in all its descendants. This naturally excludes: - **Aggregations** like `Alanine aminotransferase ... (Maximum value during study)` (`44785-4`) — out of scope. - **Non-blood specimens** such as red blood cells (`16324-6`). - **Macromolecular ALT** (`48134-1`, `100738-4`) — qualitative or research test for macro-ALT complexes. - **ALT/AST ratios** (`16325-3`, `1916-6`) — out of scope. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "ALT", "SGPT", "ALAT" or similar without further specification (most routine biochemistry analysers): `3006923 / 1742-6 — Alanine aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma`. By far the most common. - **Source explicitly states P-5'-P method**: map to `3005755 / 1743-4` (with P-5'-P) or `3027388 / 1744-2` (without P-5'-P) accordingly. Otherwise, default to `1742-6` — do **not** infer the method from the analyser brand or the country. - **Whole blood without matrix tag**: map to `46235106 / 76625-3 — Alanine aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Blood`. If the source aggregates serum/plasma and whole-blood results without a matrix tag, use `46236949 / 77144-4 — Alanine aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum, Plasma or Blood` as a generic fallback. - **Do not confuse with**: AST (aspartate aminotransferase, a related but distinct enzyme), ALT/AST ratios, macromolecular ALT, or ALT in non-blood specimens — all belong to other concept sets. - **Method is not part of the mapping target beyond P-5'-P**: when the source records additional method details (kinetic, Tris buffer, etc.), preserve them in the measurement source value — they do not change the target concept. - **Unit naming**: source systems may report `U/L` or `IU/L`. Both correspond to the same physical quantity. Map them all to the recommended unit `[U]/L` (concept 8645). Values reported in `µkat/L` should be converted using `1 U/L = 0.01667 µkat/L` (i.e. multiply µkat/L by ~60 to get U/L) [3]. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 1742-6 — Alanine aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma (Part description for "Alanine aminotransferase"). Available: https://loinc.org/1742-6/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/ [3] Joint Committee for Traceability in Laboratory Medicine (JCTLM). Database of higher-order reference measurement procedures, reference materials and reference measurement services. BIPM/IFCC/ILAC. Available: https://www.jctlmdb.org/#/app/home [4] Schumann G, Bonora R, Ceriotti F, Férard G, Ferrero CA, Franck PFH, Gella FJ, Hoelzel W, Jørgensen PJ, Kanno T, Kessner A, Klauke R, Kristiansen N, Lessinger JM, Linsinger TPJ, Misaki H, Panteghini M, Pauwels J, Schiele F, Schimmel HG, Weidemann G, Siekmann L. IFCC primary reference procedures for the measurement of catalytic activity concentrations of enzymes at 37 °C. Part 4. Reference procedure for the measurement of catalytic concentration of alanine aminotransferase. Clin Chem Lab Med. 2002;40(7):718-724. Available: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/12241021/
